bullets.text: U.S. labor demand cooled: February hires fell while job openings and separations were little changed, signaling slower churn rather than broad layoffs.
Markets rallied on expectations the U.S.–Iran conflict could de-escalate; risk-on bounce highlights how geopolitical headlines are driving short-term equity pricing.
Oil extended gains after a record monthly rally as war-related disruption risks persist; energy prices remain the key inflation and sentiment transmission channel.
Policy risk: Trump signed an executive order limiting mail-in voting, potentially reshaping 2026 election administration and intensifying federal–state governance conflict.
DHS shutdown and attempts to fund the agency via reconciliation/backdoor mechanisms raise operational and constitutional frictions, potentially weakening congressional budget control.
OpenAI reported ~$2B monthly revenue and closed a record $122B funding round, underscoring AI capital intensity, IPO expectations, and potential valuation froth.
Big Tech divergence: Microsoft logged its worst quarter since 2008 on AI concerns, showing investors are reassessing monetization timelines and AI-related capex risk.
Oracle reportedly began thousands of job cuts while ramping AI spending, a sign of cost reallocation toward compute and model infrastructure.
Nike projected further sales declines, including ~20% expected drop in China; weak outlook signals ongoing consumer softness and China demand risk for global brands.
Supply-chain security shock: Axios package compromise on NPM delivered a remote-access trojan; highlights systemic risk in open-source dependencies and software procurement.
Social Security financing gap is widening; impending trust-fund pressure forces choices between higher taxes, benefit reductions, or broader fiscal borrowing.
Science-methods warning: common lab gloves may have contaminated microplastics samples, implying some prior measurements could be biased upward and need replication.
Geopolitical risk in Lebanon rose after UN peacekeepers were killed by a roadside bomb, potentially affecting regional stability and escalation dynamics.
